old_claim.note: Bronze Working provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained because early forging/smithing is anchored to native-copper working before bronze.

source_shape.source_locator: The Anatolian copper review anchors early worked native-copper objects at Çayönü around 8200-7500 BCE, long before the corrected bronze-working node.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Early metal hammering/working is source-anchored in native copper and does not require bronze.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Bronze working is a later alloy technology; making it the prerequisite for all forging/smithing inverted the chronology.

ontology_before: Forging and Smithing treated Bronze Working as its only prerequisite.
ontology_after: Forging and Smithing is anchored to early copper working and no longer depends on later bronze working.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: Forging and Smithing must not directly depend on Bronze Working unless narrowed to bronze smithing.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to bronze smithing only.
Bronze Working is re-scoped to include all native-copper working.
A stronger source shows the Çayönü/Aşıklı evidence is not relevant to early metal hammering or smithing.
